method: ultracode workflow — per claim: 1 evidence-gathering verifier + 1 adversarial skeptic (web research on National Life/LSW primary sources), then synthesis

verdicts.appSays: FlexLife is a current NLG/LSW IUL, issued by National Life Insurance Company and/or LSW, positioned for cash-value accumulation, min face $50,000, guaranteed minimum interest rate 1.75%.
verdicts.sourcesSay: The current official National Life Group "Life Product Quick Reference Chart" (form Cat No 63236, 10-25 edition, effective Oct 2025) lists FlexLife as the lead/first product in the Indexed Universal Life section. Exact text extracted from the PDF: Issuing Company — "National Life Insurance Company (NL)" and "Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW)"; "Minimum Base Face Amount $50,000"; "Minimum Guaranteed Interest Rate — Cash value interest growth guaranteed to be at least 1.75% over life of the contract"; Product Focus — "interest growth based in part on the S&P 500, UBS Balanced Trend, and US Pacesetter... Designed for competitive income distribution." All five claim elements match verbatim.
verdicts.evidence: I fetched and byte-parsed the current guide PDF with pdfplumber (not just a search snippet). Page 1 of the IUL section confirms $50,000 base face, 1.75% lifetime minimum, NL+LSW issuers, and accumulation/income focus. One caveat: WebSearch AI-summary snippets quoted 2.5% (LSW) and 1% (NL) — but those come from SUPERSEDED brochures (forms MK8584(0911)/2014 and older FlexLife II material), not the current chart. Do not let those stale figures override the 10-25 guide; the claim uses the correct current numbers. Two minor precision notes, neither refuting the claim: (1) "1.75%" is a lifetime guaranteed minimum ("over life of the contract"), NOT an annual credited rate — indexed strategies have a separate 0% annual floor; the app should not imply clients earn 1.75%/year. (2) The $50,000 is the BASE face; the optional APB rider has a $25,000 minimum APB face — not the policy minimum. State note: LSW is not an authorized insurer in New York, so NY policies are issued by NL only; the claim's "and/or" wording accommodates this correctly.
verdicts.recommendation: No change required — all five elements are accurate against the current source. Optional precision improvement: reword "guaranteed minimum interest rate of 1.75%" to "guaranteed minimum cash-value growth of at least 1.75% over the life of the contract" so it is not mistaken for an annual credited rate.

verdicts.appSays: NLG/LSW's current IUL lineup also includes PeakLife (National Life only, HNW, min face $1M), SummitLife (LSW only, HNW, min face $1M), RapidProtect (simplified/no-exam, protection-focused, min face ~$50k), and SurvivorLife (survivorship/second-to-die, min face ~$250k).
verdicts.sourcesSay: All four products currently exist and every carrier/min-face/focus attribution matches the current official agent guide (Cat No 63236, dated 10/25), which lists the IUL lineup as FlexLife, PeakLife, RapidProtect, SummitLife, SurvivorLife. PeakLife: "National Life Insurance Company (NL) only", Min Base Face $1,000,000, focus "emerging and affluent markets with $1M+ face amounts." SummitLife: "Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) only", Min Base Face $1,000,000, focus "high-net worth clients ($1M+)." RapidProtect: Face Amounts $50,000-$500,000, Instant Decision Underwriting / no medical exam, "lifetime protection", issued by both NL and LSW, ages 0-60. SurvivorLife: "protection for two insureds" (second-to-die), Minimum Face $250,000, issued by both NL and LSW.
verdicts.evidence: Verified against the extracted full text of the primary agent guide (Cat No 63236(1025), TC8510314(1025)1). PeakLife pg 3: NL only, $1,000,000. SummitLife pg 5: LSW only, $1,000,000. RapidProtect pg 4: $50,000-$500,000, instant-decision underwriting, both carriers. SurvivorLife pg 6: two insureds, $250,000, both carriers. Cross-checked FMI product pages (SummitLife $1M, SurvivorLife $250k second-to-die) and the RapidProtect newsroom launch (Sept 2025, no medical exam, $50k-$500k). Two minor imprecisions, neither material enough to refute: (1) The guide labels PeakLife "emerging and affluent" and reserves "high-net worth" for SummitLife — calling PeakLife "HNW" is a reasonable simplification of the same $1M+ tier but is not the guide's own wording. (2) State availability: LSW is not authorized in New York, so SummitLife (LSW-only) is not available in NY; the app's SummitLife entry should carry a "não disponível em NY" note. Also note the full lineup is 5 products — the flagship FlexLife (min base face $50,000, ages 0-85, both carriers) is the one not named in this claim.
verdicts.recommendation: No change required to the core facts — carrier, min face, and focus are correct for all four. Optional tightening: (a) reword PeakLife's descriptor from "HNW" to "emerging/affluent, $1M+" to match NLG's own language (SummitLife is the "high-net worth" one); (b) add a "SummitLife not available in NY (LSW not authorized in NY)" caveat; (c) if the app lists the IUL family, add flagship FlexLife (min base face $50k, both carriers) for completeness.

verdicts.appSays: FlexLife includes FIVE ABRs at NO additional premium, paid on a DISCOUNTED basis, able to accelerate up to 100% of the death benefit subject to per-insured lifetime dollar caps: Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, Alzheimer's.
verdicts.sourcesSay: Four of the five factual assertions are confirmed by primary NLG/LSW sources; the "up to 100% of the death benefit" element is misleading. CONFIRMED: (1) The five named ABRs exist and are offered at no additional premium — NLG's resource center states "There is no additional premium for this rider" for each, and its 2023 newsroom added the Alzheimer's Disease Rider (covering Alzheimer's + Lewy Body Dementia) to the existing Terminal/Chronic/Critical Illness/Critical Injury suite. (2) Payout is discounted — NLG: "The actual payment you receive will be less than the portion of the death benefit accelerated because the benefits are paid prior to death"; the ABR Agent Guide: benefits are "based on a discounted death benefit amount (not the face value of the policy)." (3) Per-insured lifetime dollar caps exist and are rider-specific: Terminal $1,500,000; Chronic $1,500,000 ($2,000,000 in NY via ABR Chronic II); Critical Illness/Injury $1,000,000 (shared cap); Alzheimer's ~$1,500,000; reduced to $500,000 in IL/NJ for several riders. MISLEADING: "able to accelerate up to 100% of the death benefit" — (a) the client never receives 100%; the cash paid is a discounted value net of remaining premiums, fees and loans; (b) 100% single-event acceleration is realistic only for Terminal Illness — the LSW Chronic ABR is throttled to 2%/month, 24%/year up to a $360,000 annual limit, NL Chronic is capped at the IRS per-diem, and Critical Illness/Injury payouts are severity-based, so those riders cannot accelerate 100% at once; (c) the "access your FULL death benefit... up to $3 million" headline belongs to the separate Premium Chronic Care Rider added to FlexLife on Oct 29, 2025, which DOES carry an additional charge — conflating it with the no-cost suite contradicts the "no additional premium" framing.
verdicts.evidence: ABR Agent Guide (form 68928) Quick Reference: "Additional Premium Required: No"; Maximum Lifetime Benefit Per Insured — Terminal $1,500,000, Chronic $1,500,000 (NY $2,000,000), Critical $1,000,000; "ABR payout limit is based on a discounted value of the death benefit." LSW Chronic: "2% of the net death benefit each month or 24% annually, up to the annual limit of $360,000." NL Chronic annual accel = IRS per-diem ($120,450 indexed in 2014). Terminal example (Isaac) shows full-death-benefit acceleration is available, but the guide's payout is still discounted. NLG resource center confirms all riders no additional premium and discounted payout. Oct 29, 2025 newsroom: Premium Chronic Care Rider is "available at charge," up to $3M full (non-discounted) death benefit, 2%/4% monthly — a separate, extra-cost rider (not in the five), unavailable in CA/NY. Caps are also state-reduced ($500,000 in IL/NJ). Note: exact current consolidated caps rest partly on the 2014 guide plus NLG web summaries; the "$3M full DB" figure specifically belongs to the paid rider, not the five.
verdicts.recommendation: Revise the "up to 100% of the death benefit" phrase, which is the misleading element. Change to something like: "...paid on a discounted basis (the client receives less than the accelerated amount). Terminal Illness may accelerate the full death benefit; Chronic and Critical Illness/Injury are subject to monthly/annual acceleration limits. Each rider has its own per-insured lifetime cap — Terminal/Chronic/Alzheimer's $1.5M, Critical Illness/Injury $1M (state variations apply, e.g., $500k in IL/NJ)." Also add a note that the separate Premium Chronic Care Rider (up to $3M, full non-discounted death benefit) carries an additional charge and is NOT one of the five no-cost ABRs, to avoid conflating the "100%/full death benefit" language. Confirm the Alzheimer's cap and all figures against the current NLG Illustration System before client-facing use.

verdicts.appSays: Five ABR lifetime dollar caps: Terminal $1,500,000; Chronic $1,500,000; Alzheimer's $1,500,000; Critical Illness $1,000,000 and Critical Injury $1,000,000 sharing a single $1,000,000 cap — implying three independent $1.5M caps plus one shared $1M cap.
verdicts.sourcesSay: The four dollar figures are correct and the Critical pair shares one $1M cap; but Terminal/Chronic/Alzheimer's share ONE $1.5M pool rather than being three independent caps.
verdicts.evidence: Agent guide (theinsgroup PDF, confirmed by extracting text): caps table reads "Terminal: $1,500,000 / Chronic: $1,500,000 / Critical: $1,000,000 / NY Chronic only: $2,000,000"; the aggregating sentence "$1,500,000 under all contracts and all ABRs made over the entire lifetime of the insured" and "maximum lifetime limit, which applies to all policies under a single insured" prove aggregation, not per-rider independence. Two separate figures ($1.5M vs $1.0M) confirm two pools. The claim's error overstates the Terminal/Chronic/Alzheimer's ceiling roughly 3x (an agent could wrongly promise ~$4.5M instead of the true $1.5M combined). Errors of omission the claim's "verify these exact caps" invites: IL and NJ limited to $500,000; NY Chronic $2,000,000 (not $1.5M); CA requires the client to hold health insurance to include Critical Illness, Critical Injury, and Alzheimer's riders; Massachusetts restricts Chronic-Illness ABR proceeds to qualified LTC expenses. Figures verified across two independent NLG-affiliated primary documents plus NLG.com general statements.
verdicts.recommendation: Replace the "five independent caps" wording with two shared aggregate pools: (1) Terminal + Chronic + Alzheimer's = one shared $1,500,000 lifetime maximum across all ABRs and all contracts (NY: $2,000,000; IL/NJ: $500,000); (2) Critical Illness + Critical Injury = one shared $1,000,000 lifetime maximum (IL/NJ: $500,000). Add "no annual limit; a single lifetime limit applies across all ABRs per insured," plus CA (must hold health insurance) and MA (chronic proceeds restricted to LTC) caveats. Keep the four dollar figures and the critical-pair shared-cap note as-is — those parts are correct.

verdicts.appSays: "The Terminal Illness ABR triggers on a terminal illness with life expectancy of 24 months or less."
verdicts.sourcesSay: The 24-month trigger is the correct standard/default rule for both National Life (NL) and Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW). The primary NLG/LSW Accelerated Benefits Riders Agent Guide states verbatim: "Qualifications — Terminal Illness: 24 month life expectancy" and "diagnosed and are suffering from a Terminal Illness that will result in death within 24 months of certification of the terminal illness by a physician." National Life's current live resource-center page confirms: terminal illness = "an illness or chronic condition that can reasonably be expected to result in death in 24 months or less." Terminal has no waiting period, no additional premium, and a $1,500,000 lifetime acceleration cap (floor never less than $500,000). HOWEVER, the same agent guide explicitly narrows the threshold to 12 months in specific states: "(12 months in CT, PA and VT for LSW) (12 months in CT, NY and PA for NL)."
verdicts.evidence: I directly extracted text from the primary source agent-guide PDF (theinsgroup.net), which contains the exact string "Terminal Illness: 2 4 month life expectancy (12 months in CT, PA and VT for LSW) (12 months in CT, NY and PA for NL)" and the definition "will result in death within 24 months of certification of the terminal illness by a physician." The 24-month figure and the "no additional premium" / "$1,500,000 lifetime" details are independently confirmed on National Life's current live nationallife.com page ("death in 24 months or less"). The core number in the claim is correct; the claim's only defect is omitting the 12-month state variations (CT/PA/VT for LSW; CT/NY/PA for NL). Caveat: the fully parseable agent guide is a 2014-era edition (references an indexed IRS per-diem "for 2014"), so the exact 12-month state list is from an older edition and could in principle have shifted; but the 24-month core figure is verified against current live NLG language, so the claim's headline number is not outdated. No evidence of wrong carrier attribution, wrong caps, or discontinuation.
verdicts.recommendation: Keep "24 months or less" as the default — it is correct and matches current NLG language. Add a state-variation footnote: "12 meses em CT, PA e VT (LSW) e CT, NY e PA (NL)." Without it, presentations shown to clients in those four states overstate the qualifying window (the actual trigger there is a 12-month life expectancy).

verdicts.appSays: The Chronic Illness ABR triggers on inability to perform 2 of 6 ADLs for 90 days, or severe cognitive impairment, and accelerates up to ~2% of the death benefit per month.
verdicts.sourcesSay: National Life Group / LSW sources confirm every element. NLG's own definition: chronic illness = unable to perform 2 of the 6 ADLs (bathing, continence, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring) without substantial assistance for at least 90 days, OR requires substantial supervision due to severe cognitive impairment for the same period. The LSW Chronic Illness ABR accelerates 2% of the net death benefit per month = 24% annually, up to an annual limit of $360,000, and is offered at no additional premium. Multiple LSW consumer/agent guides state the same 2%/month, 24%/$360k figures.
verdicts.evidence: Trigger wording matches primary NLG language verbatim, including "severe" cognitive impairment and the six named ADLs. Monthly acceleration: 2%/month = 24%/yr, $360,000 annual cap, confirmed in the LSW ABR agent field guide and independent LSW consumer guides (marketingresources.us.com/core/lsw/LBrider.pdf; stephens-matthews.com 2019ABRConsumerGuide). "No premium for this rider" confirmed on NLG's own NWI help page. The "~2%" hedge is conservative — the actual figure is a clean 2%. Caveats that do NOT refute the claim: (1) the 2%/month formula is the LSW carrier version (NLG's other carrier, National Life Insurance Co., historically accelerates as a lump sum under the IRS per-diem limit) — but Arlindo's IUL products (FlexLife/PeakLife) are LSW, so the claim is correct in context; (2) this is the standard no-cost ABR, distinct from the optional, additional-cost Premium Chronic Care Rider (2% or 4%/month, up to 100%/$3M) — the claim describes the standard rider correctly and does not conflate them. Minor: some secondary sources phrase 90 days as a "waiting period," but primary NLG language ties the 90 days to the ADL-inability certification, exactly as the claim states.
verdicts.recommendation: No change required — the claim is accurate for the LSW Chronic Illness ABR that ships on Arlindo's IUL products. Optional tightening: qualify as "the LSW Chronic Illness ABR" and keep it visually distinct from the optional 4%/up-to-100%/$3M Premium Chronic Care Rider so the two are never conflated in the presentation.

verdicts.appSays: FlexLife offers a PAID "Premium Chronic Care Rider" accelerating up to the full death benefit (~$3,000,000 cap), ~2%-4% per month, not available in CA or NY.
verdicts.sourcesSay: National Life Group's primary newsroom page (Oct 2025 "Sandwich Generation" enhancement; rider first launched 05/31/2025) confirms the Premium Chronic Care Rider (PCC) on FlexLife IUL: it carries an ADDITIONAL charge (explicitly contrasted with the no-additional-premium ABRs); on a qualifying chronic-illness diagnosis it lets the client "potentially access their full death benefit during their lifetime (up to $3 million)"; payout is "up to 2% or 4% monthly of the death benefit, as chosen at time of issue" (subject to IRS per-diem limit; reducible but not increasable after issue; no waiting period); it is "Not available in CA or NY"; and it is underwritten by Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW). The PCC lifetime limit is independent of the other ABRs' lifetime limit.
verdicts.evidence: Every material element checks out. Carrier attribution correct (LSW, part of National Life Group). Product current and correctly named. $3M cap exact. "Up to full death benefit" exact. CA/NY exclusion exact. PAID/additional-cost framing exact. The only imprecision: the rate is a binary election of "2% OR 4% monthly, chosen at issue," not a continuous 2%-4% range — the app's "~2%-4% per month" could imply a client can pick an intermediate rate like 3%, which is not possible. The claim's "roughly/~" hedges this, and no figure is wrong, so this is a wording nuance rather than a factual error. Min face amount to elect the rider was not stated in the sources reviewed (unverifiable, but the app does not assert one).
verdicts.recommendation: Reword "~2%-4% per month" to "2% or 4% per month (elected at issue)" to avoid implying a client can select an intermediate rate. All other figures (paid rider, up to full death benefit, ~$3M cap, not available in CA/NY) require no change.

verdicts.appSays: FlexLife includes at no cost a Value-Added Services Rider (caregiver support via Homethrive, not available in NY) and a Fertility Journey Rider (accumulated-value credit after eligible fertility treatment).
verdicts.sourcesSay: National Life primary sources confirm both riders exactly as the app describes.
verdicts.evidence: Verified directly against primary sources. nationallife.com Homethrive page: "at no additional cost to policyholders," "There's no extra charge for this rider," "all new eligible 2025 National Life Group FlexLife Indexed Universal Life (IUL) policies," Homethrive "independent of National Life Group," "Not available" in NY, CA/FL/ND get services without the rider. nationallife.com fertility page: rider "Automatically attached to all new eligible FlexLife policies, depending on state approval," "one-time, lump-sum Accumulation Value Credit." Carrier attribution correct (National Life Group / LSW; FlexLife is their IUL). The app copy does not attach any dollar figure to the fertility credit, so it avoids the one way this could go wrong. No overstated cap, no wrong carrier, no discontinued/renamed product, no "up to 100%" overreach. The only imprecision is an omission, not an error: the claim doesn't note the CA/FL/ND variation for the caregiver rider.
verdicts.recommendation: No change required. Optional precision upgrade: for the Value-Added Services Rider, add that in CA/FL/ND the caregiver services are provided without the rider on the policy (still NY-excluded). Never render the Fertility Journey credit as a fixed dollar amount — keep describing it as a one-time premium-based accumulation-value credit.

verdicts.appSays: FlexLife offers optional paid riders: a Waiver of Monthly Deductions Rider (waives monthly deductions on total disability) and a Children's Term Rider covering $5,000–$25,000 per child to age 23 at a single rate regardless of number of children.
verdicts.sourcesSay: Verified against the current FlexLife Product Guide and confirmed via the NLG Life Product Reference Chart.
verdicts.evidence: Downloaded and text-extracted the current agent product guide directly (curl → pdfplumber). Cover page ID: TC8797579(0326)1 / Cat No 104727(0126) = current Jan 2026 edition. P.27 CTR text explicitly says "until they reach age 25" and the coverage is convertible for six times face "when the child reaches age 25." The app's "to age 23" matches only the discontinued original 2014 FlexLife guide (66346 MK8584); every current/intermediate edition uses age 25. This is the single factual error. Dollar range ($5,000–$25,000), single-rate/level-per-$1,000 structure, the WMD total-disability trigger, and the paid/optional framing are all verbatim-confirmed in the current source. Carrier attribution (National Life Group) is correct. Note: neither rider claims "up to 100%" or "no cost" — no such framing to refute here; the no-cost living-benefit framing applies to the separate Accelerated Benefits Rider, not these two.
verdicts.recommendation: Change "to age 23" → "to age 25" in the Children's Term Rider description. This is the only outright error. Recommended additions for a client-facing tool: note that both the Children's Term Rider and Waiver of Monthly Deductions Rider are "não disponível em Nova York (NY)", and optionally that CTR coverage is sold in $1,000 increments (multiplos de US$ 1.000).

verdicts.appSays: FlexLife includes a Lifetime Income Benefit Rider (LIBR) that is auto-added at no cost to add (a charge applies only during payout), generating a guaranteed income stream that can pay for life to approximately age 120.
verdicts.sourcesSay: NLG primary sources confirm 3 of 4 elements verbatim. FlexLife Product Guide (form 66346, National Life Insurance Company / LSW): "This rider does not have a charge until exercised and will be automatically added to all eligible new issue policies" and "There is a monthly charge from the accumulated value during the income payment period." Income guarantee: "Once exercised, LIBR guarantees your clients a stream of income for the life of the insured - money that cannot be outlived" and "Lifetime Income, guaranteed, once the rider has been exercised." The word "120" appears ZERO times in the 37-page guide; LIBR is consistently described as paying "for the life of the insured," a mortality-based benefit, NOT "to age 120." The guarantee is conditioned on the rider being exercised (insured age 60-85; current-form materials require the policy in force ~10 years). LIBR is a current FlexLife feature (National Life newsroom, Oct 29, 2025, form 20266(0614)).
verdicts.evidence: Auto-add and no-charge-to-add mechanics are confirmed word-for-word, as is the payout-period charge and the "income for life, guaranteed once exercised" benefit. The single defect is "to approximately age 120": no primary NLG rider or product document ties age 120 to LIBR (0 occurrences of "120" in the product guide; no "maturity"/"endow" language for LIBR). Age 120 is only the industry-standard IUL policy maturity/illustration horizon, not a LIBR term. Stating income "can pay for life to approximately age 120" both attributes an unsupported figure to the rider and understates a true lifetime benefit by implying it stops at 120. Secondary issue: "guaranteed income stream" omits the material "once exercised" condition (benefit is not guaranteed until LIBR is exercised; guarantees subject to the carrier's claims-paying ability). Independently verified: carrier attribution correct (National Life Insurance Company / LSW), floors $15,000 death benefit / $1,000 CSV correct, "LSW is not an authorized insurer in New York" (so LSW FlexLife unavailable in NY). Note: a WebFetch AI summary that claimed the rider "terminates at age 120" and is "available in New York" was a hallucination contradicted by the raw extracted PDF text.
verdicts.recommendation: Remove "to approximately age 120." Replace "generating a guaranteed income stream that can pay for life to approximately age 120" with wording that (a) drops the age-120 figure (or labels it only as the illustration horizon, not a LIBR term) and (b) adds the "once exercised" qualifier. Suggested pt-BR-ready copy: "gera um fluxo de renda que, uma vez acionado o rider (segurado entre 60 e 85 anos e apolice em vigor ha pelo menos 10 anos), e garantido por toda a vida - renda que o cliente nao pode sobreviver." If an age-120 horizon must be shown, frame it strictly as the illustration/policy-maturity endpoint, not as when LIBR income stops.

verdicts.appSays: FlexLife index accounts have a 0% floor (no loss in down years); the max illustrated rate is limited by AG 49-A; ~6.5% is a reasonable current ceiling.
verdicts.sourcesSay: FlexLife has a built-in 0% floor (1% on one strategy); illustrations are governed by AG 49-B (effective May 1, 2023, superseding AG 49-A); top max illustrated rate ~6.42%.
verdicts.evidence: Three-part claim. (1) 0% floor: ACCURATE but incomplete — standard strategies use a 0% floor; one strategy (S&P 500 1% Floor, 6.50% cap) uses a 1% floor; and "no loss" overstates it since monthly policy charges (COI) continue in flat years, so account value can still decline. (2) "AG 49-A": INCORRECT/OUTDATED — NAIC AG 49-B is effective May 1, 2023 and governs current IUL illustrations (confirmed by multiple industry sources: FIG Marketing, InsuranceNewsNet, SOA; and a real AG-49-B-era FlexLife illustration). This is a substantive factual error. (3) "~6.5% ceiling": ACCURATE/defensible — top strategy max illustrated rate is 6.42% (a Jan 2023 pre-AG-49-B bulletin listed 5.89%-5.92%); rates are non-guaranteed, reset by LSW, per-strategy, and NY is priced differently, so it should be framed as approximate/current. Also worth noting: the carrier is LSW, not National Life Insurance Company (both under the National Life Group umbrella).
verdicts.recommendation: Change "Actuarial Guideline 49-A (AG49-A)" to "Actuarial Guideline 49-B (AG 49-B)". Optionally tighten the copy to: "FlexLife index accounts have a 0% floor (1% floor available on one strategy; ongoing policy charges still apply in flat years). Illustrated rates are capped by AG 49-B; the current maximum illustrated rate tops out around 6.4% on the highest-participation uncapped strategies (lower on capped strategies) and is non-guaranteed, set by LSW."

verdicts.appSays: National Life issues USD IUL policies to foreign nationals incl. Brazilian residents ("B" country), requiring US nexus, US bank account, US signing/delivery, US solicitation and illustration, and a higher ~$500,000 minimum face for FN cases vs the $50,000 domestic FlexLife minimum.
verdicts.sourcesSay: The current NLG Foreign National Guidelines (Cat No 69798, rev code 1125 = Nov 2025; footer still reads "Last Updated June 2022") confirm essentially every element verbatim. Carrier: National Life Group = National Life Insurance Company (Montpelier, VT) and Life Insurance Company of the Southwest / LSW (Addison, TX); products "priced for the U.S. population." Permanent products only (IUL qualifies). Brazil is explicitly listed under B Countries (page 7). Face amount minimum: $500,000 (page 4). US nexus required — own US real property/business, married to a US-citizen US resident, F-visa student, min $100,000 documented US assets held >=3 months, or EB-5; "National Life Group will not accept foreign nationals that do not have a nexus to the U.S." All premium from a U.S. bank in the owner's/insured's name (cash equivalents not accepted). "Application and underwriting requirements must be completed, signed, and dated in the U.S." Policy "must be delivered in the U.S." and accepted in the US. "All solicitation and sales activities must take place in the U.S.; no marketing or policy materials may be transmitted or delivered outside the U.S." FlexLife domestic minimum base face amount confirmed at $50,000 via the Life Product Quick Reference Chart (Cat 63236) and product guides.
verdicts.evidence: Verified against the primary PDF (fetched and read in full). Every dollar figure and classification is exact: $500,000 FN min face (page 4), Brazil under B Countries (page 7), $50,000 FlexLife domestic min (Quick Reference Chart / product guides). US bank, US signing/dating, US delivery/acceptance, and US solicitation are all quoted verbatim. USD is implicit but factually certain (US-priced products, US bank required, US delivery). One soft spot: the word "illustration" does NOT appear verbatim as an act that must "occur in the US" — the document instead bans transmitting/delivering any "marketing or policy materials" outside the US and requires all sales activities in the US, which substantively covers illustrations but is an inference, not a direct quote. I also caught and discarded a WebSearch summary error that conflated the nexus threshold as "$500,000 for 3 months"; the primary source clearly states $100,000 for at least 3 months (the $500,000 figure is the face-amount minimum, a different line item). Not part of the claim but worth surfacing if the app expands: B countries cap at "Preferred" rate class (Elite is A-only); $30M max face (autobind 18-70), $20M (71-75); ABR/living-benefit riders require the client to be in the US obtaining US-based health care; premium financing is a separate track (global net worth >$10M, >=$1M US liquid assets); RapidProtect is not FN-eligible; OFAC/PEP and various occupation exclusions apply.
verdicts.recommendation: No change required — the claim is accurate. Optional precision: soften "illustration occurring in the US" to reflect that the guidelines require all solicitation/sales activities in the US and prohibit transmitting marketing or policy materials outside the US (the "illustration" wording is an accurate inference, not a verbatim term). If the app markets living benefits (ABR riders) to Brazilian clients, add the constraint that the client must be in the US obtaining US-based health care to accelerate, and that B countries are limited to the "Preferred" rate class.

synthesisReport:
# Arlindo — FlexLife IUL Factual Accuracy: Correction Report

## 1. Overall verdict
Strong overall: 6 of 12 claims are fully accurate and 6 are PARTIAL (correct core facts with specific misstatements or missing state caveats) — **no fully refuted claims**, but 4 substantive factual errors must be fixed before client-facing use, plus several compliance/state caveats.

---

## 2. FIX LIST — prioritized (PARTIAL claims first)

### P1 — `abr-caps`: ABR lifetime caps are wrongly shown as independent (overstates ceiling ~3x)
- **App says:** Terminal $1.5M, Chronic $1.5M, Alzheimer's $1.5M, Critical Illness $1M, Critical Injury $1M — implying three independent $1.5M caps + one shared $1M cap.
- **Sources say:** Terminal + Chronic + Alzheimer's **share ONE $1,500,000 aggregate pool** across all ABRs and all contracts per insured; Critical Illness + Critical Injury share ONE $1,000,000 pool. Independent-cap wording could let an agent wrongly promise ~$4.5M instead of the true $1.5M combined.
- **Change:** Replace with two shared pools: (1) Terminal + Chronic + Alzheimer's = one shared **$1,500,000** lifetime max (NY: **$2,000,000**; IL/NJ: **$500,000**); (2) Critical Illness + Critical Injury = one shared **$1,000,000** (IL/NJ: $500,000). Add "no annual limit; single lifetime limit per insured across all ABRs." Keep the four dollar figures — they are correct.

### P2 — `abr-suite`: "accelerate up to 100% of the death benefit" is misleading
- **App says:** Five no-cost ABRs can accelerate up to 100% of the death benefit.
- **Sources say:** Client never receives 100% (payout is **discounted**, net of premiums/fees/loans). 100% single-event acceleration is realistic only for **Terminal**; Chronic is throttled (LSW 2%/mo, 24%/yr, $360k annual cap; NL to IRS per-diem) and Critical Illness/Injury are severity-based. The "full DB up to $3M" language belongs to the separate **paid** Premium Chronic Care Rider — conflating it breaks the "no additional premium" framing.
- **Change:** "...paid on a discounted basis (client receives less than the accelerated amount). Terminal Illness may accelerate the full death benefit; Chronic and Critical Illness/Injury are subject to monthly/annual limits. Per-insured lifetime caps apply (see P1)." Add a note that Premium Chronic Care (up to $3M, full non-discounted DB) is a separate paid rider, NOT one of the five.

### P3 — `libr`: "pays to approximately age 120" attributes an unsupported figure
- **App says:** LIBR auto-added, no cost to add, guaranteed income stream that can pay "for life to approximately age 120."
- **Sources say:** Auto-add, no charge-to-add, payout-period charge, and lifetime-income mechanics are confirmed verbatim. But "120" appears **zero times** in the product guide — LIBR pays "for the life of the insured." Age 120 is only the IUL illustration/maturity horizon. Also missing the material **"once exercised"** condition (insured age 60–85; policy in force ~10 yrs).
- **Change:** Remove "to approximately age 120." Use: "gera um fluxo de renda que, uma vez acionado o rider (segurado entre 60 e 85 anos e apólice em vigor há pelo menos 10 anos), é garantido por toda a vida." Only show age 120 if explicitly labeled as the illustration horizon.

### P4 — `ag49a-floor`: cites the superseded actuarial guideline
- **App says:** Max illustrated rate limited by **AG 49-A**; 0% floor; ~6.5% ceiling.
- **Sources say:** **AG 49-B** is in effect (May 1, 2023) and governs current IUL illustrations — AG 49-A is a substantive factual error. 0% floor is correct but incomplete (one strategy uses a 1% floor; "no loss" overstates it since monthly COI charges continue in flat years). ~6.5% is defensible (top strategy ~6.42%). Carrier is **LSW**, not National Life Insurance Company.
- **Change:** "AG 49-A" → "**AG 49-B**". Add: "0% floor (1% on one strategy; policy charges still apply in flat years); max illustrated rate ~6.4% on highest-participation strategies, non-guaranteed, set by LSW."

### P5 — `waiver-childrens`: Children's Term Rider age is wrong
- **App says:** Children's Term Rider covers $5,000–$25,000 per child **to age 23**.
- **Sources say:** Current Jan 2026 product guide says coverage runs **to age 25** (convertible for 6× face at age 25). "Age 23" matches only the discontinued 2014 guide. Dollar range, single-rate structure, WMD trigger all confirmed.
- **Change:** "to age 23" → "**to age 25**". Add: both CTR and Waiver of Monthly Deductions are "não disponível em NY"; optionally CTR sold in múltiplos de US$ 1.000.

### P6 — `terminal-terms`: missing 12-month state variations
- **App says:** Terminal Illness ABR triggers at life expectancy of **24 months or less**.
- **Sources say:** 24 months is the correct default, but the threshold is **12 months in CT, PA, VT (LSW) and CT, NY, PA (NL)**. Presentations in those states currently overstate the qualifying window.
- **Change:** Keep "24 meses ou menos" as default; add footnote: "12 meses em CT, PA e VT (LSW) e CT, NY e PA (NL)."

### P7 — `premium-chronic-care`: rate range implies an impossible intermediate election
- **App says:** ~2%–4% per month.
- **Sources say:** It is a **binary election of 2% OR 4% monthly, chosen at issue** — no intermediate rate (e.g., 3%). All other figures (paid rider, up to full DB, ~$3M cap, not available CA/NY) are exact.
- **Change:** "~2%–4% per month" → "**2% ou 4% por mês (escolhido na emissão)**".

### Minor precision (optional, in CONFIRMED claims)
- `flexlife-basics`: reword "guaranteed minimum interest rate 1.75%" → "**crescimento garantido do valor em dinheiro de no mínimo 1,75% ao longo da vida do contrato**" (it is a lifetime minimum, not an annual credited rate; indexed strategies have a separate 0% annual floor).
- `lineup`: PeakLife's own NLG descriptor is "emerging/affluent, $1M+" (SummitLife is the "high-net-worth" one); add "SummitLife não disponível em NY (LSW não autorizada em NY)."
- `chronic-terms`: label as "the LSW Chronic Illness ABR" and keep visually distinct from the paid Premium Chronic Care Rider.
- `value-added-fertility`: note CA/FL/ND get caregiver services without the rider (still NY-excluded); never show the fertility credit as a fixed dollar amount.

## 3. COMPLIANCE-SENSITIVE — verify prominently

- **`foreign-national` (CONFIRMED, accurate):** For Brazilian ("B" country) clients — **$500,000 minimum face** for FN cases vs. $50,000 domestic FlexLife; requires US nexus, US bank account (owner/insured name, no cash equivalents), US signing/dating, US delivery/acceptance, and **all solicitation/sales activity in the US** (no marketing/policy materials transmitted outside the US). One nuance: "illustration must occur in the US" is an accurate inference, not a verbatim term — soften to "todas as atividades de venda nos EUA." If marketing living benefits to Brazilian clients, add: client must be **in the US obtaining US-based health care** to accelerate an ABR, and B countries are capped at the **"Preferred"** rate class. LSW is not authorized in NY (LSW-issued FlexLife/SummitLife unavailable there).
- **State-availability caveats to surface app-wide (from P1/P5/P6 above):** NY (LSW products, CTR/WMD, PCC rider all excluded/restricted), CA (PCC excluded; ABR Critical/Alzheimer's require client to hold health insurance), IL/NJ ($500k ABR caps), MA (chronic ABR proceeds restricted to LTC expenses).

## 4. Confirmed accurate (no change required)
- `flexlife-basics` — NL/LSW issuer, $50k min base face, 1.75% lifetime minimum, accumulation/income focus.
- `lineup` — PeakLife (NL, $1M), SummitLife (LSW, $1M), RapidProtect ($50k–$500k, no-exam, both carriers), SurvivorLife (second-to-die, $250k, both carriers).
- `chronic-terms` — 2 of 6 ADLs / 90 days or severe cognitive impairment; ~2%/mo (LSW).
- `premium-chronic-care` — paid rider, up to full DB, ~$3M, not in CA/NY, LSW (fix only the rate-range wording, P7).
- `value-added-fertility` — Homethrive caregiver rider (no cost, not in NY) + Fertility Journey accumulation-value credit.
- `foreign-national` — all figures exact (see compliance section).

## 5. Needs human / current-brochure check before client use
- **ABR consolidated caps & Alzheimer's cap** — several figures rest partly on the 2014-era ABR agent guide plus NLG web summaries; confirm current $1.5M/$1M pools and Alzheimer's cap against the live NLG Illustration System.
- **Terminal 12-month state list** — verified only against the 2014-era guide (24-month core is confirmed current); confirm the exact CT/PA/VT/NY list against the current edition.
- **PCC minimum face to elect** — not stated in sources reviewed (app does not assert one; fine as-is, but confirm if adding).
- **Current max illustrated rate** (~6.42%) — non-guaranteed, LSW-reset, per-strategy, NY priced differently; verify the live number and always frame as approximate/current.
